trade.summary: Cina is Corea del Sud's largest trading partner. Corea del Sud exports semiconductors, petrochemicals, vehicles, and displays. Cina exports electronics, machinery, and chemicals. The trade relationship is vital but increasingly tense due to competition in key industries (displays, batteries, EVs) and security tensions.
disputes: THAAD deployment fallout (2016-2017) ongoing economic effects
Corea del Sudn concerns about Chinese competition in chips, displays, and EVs
Import restrictions on K-culture (Hallyu) during THAAD dispute
Technology competition in batteries and semiconductors

diplomatic.summary: Relations remain significantly strained following the THAAD controversy of 2016-2017. Cina imposed informal economic sanctions (tourism boycott, retail restrictions on Corea del Sudn brands). Relations formally normalized but distrust persists. Corea del Sud under President Yoon (2022-2025) tilted strongly toward the Stati Uniti-Giappone alliance, deepening Cina's concerns. Corea del Sud's involvement in semiconductor export controls has further strained ties.
recent_incidents: THAAD economic retaliation legacy still affecting Corea del Sudn tourism to Cina
Corea del Sud joined Stati Uniti-led semiconductor export controls
North Corea del Sud-Cina alignment vs Corea del Sud-Stati Uniti-Giappone alignment deepening
Corea del Sudn domestic impeachment crisis (2024-2025) created uncertainty

military.summary: Firmly in opposing alignments. Corea del Sud is a Stati Uniti treaty ally with 28,500 Stati Uniti troops on its soil. Cina objects to Stati Uniti military presence and THAAD. Cina-North Corea del Sud alignment means Cina is, in effect, backing Corea del Sud's primary adversary. No arms transfers or joint exercises.
incidents: China opposes THAAD missile defense system as anti-China
China backs North Korea diplomatically, limiting UN sanctions effectiveness
Chinese military exercises and overflights near Korean peninsula

financial.summary: Significant bilateral investment declining. Chinese investment in Corea del Sud is limited. Corea del Sudn conglomerates (Samsung, SK, LG, Hyundai) have large Cina operations but are diversifying to Vietnam, India. Cina informally targeted Corea del Sudn brands (Lotte, Hyundai) during THAAD dispute. Investment flows becoming more cautious.

energy.summary: Corea del Sud imports Chinese solar panels and battery materials. Some LNG trade. Corea del Sud is reducing rare earth dependency on Cina. Chinese-Corea del Sudn competition in battery supply chains intensifying.

scientific.summary: Moderate scientific cooperation in materials science, display technology, and chemistry. Corea del Sudn universities maintain Chinese partnerships. Growing scrutiny as Corea del Sud aligns more closely with Stati Uniti technology frameworks. Joint publications declining from previous highs.
